commands.name: git init
commands.notes: Creates a new .git folder and begins tracking changes.

commands.name: git clone
commands.description: Copy a repository from a remote source.
commands.notes: Create a local copy of an existing project.

commands.name: git add
commands.description: Stage changes to be included in the next commit.
commands.notes: Add modified or new files to the staging area.

commands.name: git commit
commands.description: Record a snapshot of staged changes.
commands.notes: Commits should have clear, descriptive messages.

commands.name: git push
commands.description: Upload local commits to a remote repository.
commands.notes: Share your work with others by pushing to a remote.

commands.name: git pull
commands.description: Fetch and merge changes from a remote repository.
commands.notes: Keep your local branch up to date with remote changes.

commands.name: git branch
commands.notes: Use branches to isolate work and avoid conflicts.

commands.name: git merge
commands.description: Combine changes from another branch into the current branch.
commands.notes: Merging integrates completed work back into the main branch.

commands.name: git checkout
commands.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files.
commands.notes: Switch to another branch to work on a different task.

commands.name: git status
commands.description: Show the status of the working directory and staging area.
commands.notes: Use this command frequently to see what is staged, modified, or untracked.
